Title: Kubernetes, explained — interactive walkthroughs

URL Source: https://explained.kubesimplify.com/

Markdown Content:
Interactive Kubernetes, explained

## What actually happens 

inside Kubernetes.

Four guided animations that unpack the Kubernetes control plane — from a single kubectl run to the packets moving through an ingress controller. Play, pause, dive deeper.

[23 steps · ~7 min From command to running pod kubectl run nginx Every step kubectl, API server, etcd, scheduler, kubelet, and containerd take to get a pod from request to Running. Start scenario](https://explained.kubesimplify.com/pod)

[12 steps · ~4 min Filter → Score → Bind The scheduler, inside out How kube-scheduler picks a node: informer queues, filter plugins, score plugins, and the final bind. Start scenario](https://explained.kubesimplify.com/scheduler)

[10 steps · ~6 min Selector → EndpointSlice → kernel How a Service finds your Pods Three controllers cooperate to turn a label selector into a working ClusterIP. Verified against kubernetes/kubernetes 1.36 source. Start scenario](https://explained.kubesimplify.com/service)

[7 steps · ~5 min How Services scale to thousands of pods Inside an EndpointSlice Why the legacy Endpoints API died, what is in a slice, the three conditions that decide who gets traffic, and how topology hints route zone-aware. Verified against kubernetes/kubernetes 1.36 and CoreDNS 1.11 source. Start scenario](https://explained.kubesimplify.com/endpointslices)

[7 steps · ~5 min iptables · IPVS · nftables kube-proxy modes, inside out How kube-proxy turns virtual-IP Services and EndpointSlices into node-level packet-processing rules. Three modes, three packet paths, three scale curves. Verified against kubernetes/kubernetes 1.36 source. Start scenario](https://explained.kubesimplify.com/kube-proxy)

[10 steps · ~4 min Internet → DNS → LB → pod A request through ingress Follow an HTTP request from a browser to the container — through DNS, load balancer, ingress controller, service, iptables, veth, and finally your pod. Start scenario](https://explained.kubesimplify.com/ingress)

[Explorable map Every component, every link Anatomy of a cluster An interactive map of a Kubernetes cluster. Hover any component to learn what it does, who it talks to, and where it lives. Start scenario](https://explained.kubesimplify.com/cluster)

[Watch the video walkthroughs](https://youtube.com/@kubesimplify)
Keyboard: ← → step · space play · 1-9 jump
